sharedRootCause: The Property-tab reconciled facts (report.reconciliation.acreage, built in routes.ts projectPublicScreening) applied a private 15% assessed-vs-mapped threshold, while the operator record, the acreage-basis record, and the deal-card audit used a 5% / 0.1 ac materiality. Card 19's 1.32-vs-1.15 gap is 12.9% — below 15%, above 5% — so exactly one subsystem reported the acreage as reconciled/unconflicted while the header, acreage-basis panel, value readiness, and the audit's acreage_conflict_preserved check all flagged a conflict. The reconciliation acreage fact was derived from ad-hoc thresholds instead of the shared canonical acreage basis.
whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: Existing reconciliation tests exercised large-parcel conflicts well above 15% (e.g. 17.93 vs 17.67 on ~18 ac) and never a sub-15% but material small-parcel gap, so the threshold divergence between subsystems was never asserted. No test tied report.reconciliation.acreage.conflict to operatorRecord.identity.acreageConflict for the same inputs.
whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The prior card was accepted before the canonical acreage-basis record existed; there was no cross-tab acreage-consistency journey, and the automated acreage-conflict journey's fixture selector only scanned the first 8 deal cards on the base /deal-cards/:id route (the conflict data lives on /report), so card 19 was never exercised.
missingInvariant: report.reconciliation.acreage.conflict MUST equal operatorRecord.identity.acreageBasis.disputed for the same card; a spatial overlay's acreage MUST NOT exceed the mapped GIS geometry it was sampled against; both are now derived from the single canonical acreage basis (5% / 0.1 ac materiality).
missingAcceptanceJourney: A cross-tab acreage-consistency journey on a small-parcel sub-15% conflict fixture (card 19 / 200 Sid Edens Rd): header CONFLICT, acreage-basis panel, value-readiness blocked, Property-tab reconciled facts conflicted, flood overlay acreage <= mapped geometry, and audit acreage_conflict_preserved passing.
sharedRepair: routes.ts projectPublicScreening now builds the acreage fact from the shared canonical basis via acreageFactFromBasis(buildAcreageBasis(...)) instead of the private 15% gate; the operator record pins flood overlay acreage to mapped-geometry x parcelPercentage; and public-property-intelligence-live samples overlay area against the queried geometry, not provider/assessed acres.
newRegressionTest: src/landos/deal-card-reconciliation.test.ts 'acreageFactFromBasis — reconciled facts consume the canonical basis (WS1 F2/F3 regression)': asserts a 12.9% gap yields conflict=true/needs_confirmation with both bases preserved, agreement yields reconciled, and empty basis yields null. Plus acreage-basis.test.ts checkOverlayConsistency overlay-exceeds-geometry cases.
newBrowserAssertion: On the acceptance-example card: Property-tab reconciled Acreage shows a CONFLICT (not a single reconciled number), the FEMA flood overlay acreage does not exceed the mapped geometry acreage, and the executive audit shows acreage_conflict_preserved passing (no 'need repair' banner for acreage).
